message:
feat(ui): expand theme system to 18 presets and remove custom color overrides

Major overhaul of the style system theme infrastructure:

Theme preset expansion (6 -> 18 presets):
- Rename Dark -> TokyoNight and Light -> Daylight with serde aliases for
  backward compatibility (existing "dark"/"light" config values still parse)
- Rename Catppuccin display name to "Catppuccin Mocha" for precision
- Add 12 new community-beloved presets: SolarizedDark, SolarizedLight,
  Monokai, GruvboxDark, OneDark, RosePine, Everforest, Kanagawa,
  AyuMirage, Nightfox, CyberpunkAurora, Synthwave84
- Each preset has a full 19-slot color definition (primary, secondary,
  accent, background, surface, overlay, text, text_muted, text_subtle,
  success, warning, error, info, border, border_focused, selection_bg,
  selection_fg, scrollbar_track, scrollbar_thumb)
- All presets are WCAG AA contrast-checked via the existing test suite
- SolarizedLight joins Daylight as a light-mode preset (dark_mode=false)

Remove ThemeColorOverrides system:
- Delete the ThemeColorOverrides struct (19 optional hex color slots)
- Delete validate(), is_empty(), validate_color_slot(), parse_color_slot(),
  parse_hex_color(), and nibble_to_u8() helper functions
- Remove the colors field from ThemeConfig
- Remove the InvalidColorValue variant from ThemeConfigError
- Simplify StyleContext::from_options_with_theme_config to no longer
  return Result (infallible now that color validation is gone)
- Simplify StyleContext::build to no longer accept overrides parameter

The rationale: with 18 high-quality presets covering every major editor
theme family, the custom hex-override system added complexity (validation,
WCAG checking, error paths) that was rarely used.  Users can now cycle
through presets with F2/Shift+F2 instead of hand-editing hex codes.

Update all tests to reference TokyoNight/Daylight instead of Dark/Light,
and add parse tests for new preset aliases (gruvbox, rose-pine, etc.).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
